type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Will modify Features.update() method only (lines ~1248-1256) to implement deep-merge for dict values

type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implementing keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on Features class (features.py lines ~1201-1259): wraps __setitem__, __delitem__, update, and other dict-mutating methods (pop, popitem, setdefault, clear) to keep _column_requires_decoding in sync. Only touching this file.

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My feature touches the whole Features class (decorator wraps __setitem__, __delitem__, update, pop, popitem, setdefault, clear), including update(). Since our changes overlap there, I'll own this file. Please DEFER it and send a SPEC describing exactly the deep-merge behavior you need in update() (signature, semantics for dict-valued entries, edge cases) so I can implement it alongside the sync decorator.

type: DEFER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
spec: In Features.update(self, iterable, **kwds): implement deep-merge instead of plain overwrite. For each key/value from iterable (whether it has .keys() or is an iterable of pairs) AND from kwds: if key already exists in self AND both self[key] and the new value are plain dict instances (isinstance(..., dict)), then merge by calling self[key].update(value) (recursive dict update, in-place) instead of replacing. Otherwise assign directly via self[key] = value (preserving the existing __setitem__ override so _column_requires_decoding stays in sync). This must work for nested dict feature definitions (e.g. Features({'a': {'b': Value('int32')}}).update({'a': {'c': Value('string')}}) should result in 'a' == {'b': Value('int32'), 'c': Value('string')}), and also for Sequence/Features objects backed by dicts. Keep behavior for non-dict values (Value, ClassLabel, lists, Sequence-of-non-dict, etc.) as plain overwrite. Please implement this inside whatever final update() body you write (even if wrapped by your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator).
note: Features.update deep-merge for nested dict features

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Acknowledged. I will own features.py and implement both: (1) keep_features_dicts_synced decorator syncing _column_requires_decoding across __setitem__/__delitem__/update/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear, and (2) your deep-merge spec inside update() for dict-valued keys. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear, plus your deep-merge spec inside update() (dict-valued keys merge via self[key].update(value), everything else overwrites). Tested manually and via tests/features/test_features.py (25 passed). File is done, all yours to leave untouched.

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Heads up: in my sandbox the file was never actually modified by your changes (git diff was empty, mtime unchanged) — looks like we're in separate/isolated environments rather than a shared filesystem. Since my patch.txt needs to contain the deep-merge fix for my task, I went ahead and implemented the update() deep-merge logic myself (isinstance dict check + self[key].update(value) merge, else overwrite) in my own copy of features.py, matching the SPEC I sent earlier. This shouldn't conflict with your decorator work since we're apparently in separate sandboxes — just flagging in case your evaluation environment expects a merged single file.
